name: Petefish, Skiles & Co.
description_short: Community bank founded in 1870 in Virginia, Illinois, offering deposit products, loans, and online banking across multiple counties.
description_long:
Petefish, Skiles & Co. Bank is one of the oldest continuously operating banks in Illinois, with roots dating back to August 1, 1870, when it opened as a private banking firm in Virginia, Illinois. The bank survived the Great Depression with only a brief closure during FDR's 1932 Bank Moratorium and has grown through strategic acquisitions over 150+ years. Today it operates as a full-service community bank serving customers across multiple locations in central Illinois.

The bank offers standard retail banking services including deposit products (checking, savings, CDs implied by "Deposit Products" menu), personal and commercial loans, online banking with digital tools like ClickSWITCH for easy account switching, and mobile banking capabilities. Customers can open accounts online from home and access loan payment services through their web platform. The bank maintains multiple branch locations across Cass County and surrounding areas with published rates and hours.

Petefish, Skiles & Co. distinguishes itself through its exceptional longevity and local community presence spanning 150+ years. Unlike newer banks, it has demonstrated resilience through multiple economic crises and has organically grown through acquisitions of failed or consolidating regional banks (Farmers National Bank 1918, Centennial National Bank 1930, Peoples Bank 1936, Illini Bank branches 2000, People's State Bank 2007, First National Bank of Beardstown 2024). This track record suggests stability and deep roots in the communities it serves.

The main limitation is that Petefish, Skiles & Co. operates primarily as a regional community bank with limited geographic footprint, making it suitable only for customers in central Illinois. The website provides minimal detail about specific loan products, rates, deposit account features, or competitive advantages. No information is available regarding minimum deposit requirements, fee structures, APYs, loan terms, or credit requirements—essential details for consumer comparison shopping. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking platform with login and enrollment
Multiple branch locations with published hours
pros: Over 150 years of continuous banking history (founded 1870) demonstrates institutional stability
Survived the Great Depression with minimal disruption (only brief 1932 closure), showing proven financial resilience
Online account opening available from home for convenience
ClickSWITCH tool enables fast, secure, free switching of direct deposits and automatic payments
Mobile banking and online banking platforms available for digital customers
Multiple branch locations across central Illinois including recent 2024 acquisition expanding service area
Community banking focus with local decision-making (not a national chain)
cons: Website lacks transparent fee schedules, making cost comparison difficult
No specific APY or interest rate information published for deposit accounts
Limited details on loan products, terms, rates, or qualification requirements
Geographic service area restricted to central Illinois (not suitable for non-local customers)
Minimal information about deposit insurance coverage, minimum balances, or account features beyond menu labels
best_for: Residents of central Illinois (Virginia, Tallula, Petersburg, Greenview, Chandlerville, Havana, Jacksonville, Beardstown, Winchester, Murrayville) seeking community banking relationships
Customers prioritizing bank stability and local ownership over national brand convenience
Rural and small-town Illinois customers underserved by larger regional or national banks
diagnosis: Petefish, Skiles & Co. is best for customers in central Illinois seeking a long-established, community-focused bank with proven stability through economic crises. The main caveat is the complete lack of pricing transparency on the website—customers must contact the bank directly to learn about rates, fees, minimum deposits, and specific loan terms before making any decisions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
